candidate_title: Sulfide Electrolyte Thermal Runaway Suppression via Phase Transition
key_flaw: none
reviewer_note: Highly specific mechanism: endothermic PCM encapsulant absorbing latent heat at 60C onset. Directly addresses the thermal runaway problem limiting solid-state battery commercialization. Testable prediction (zero runaway events in short-circuit tests). Strong IP position.

candidate_title: Hydrogen Electrolysis Waste Heat Recovery Loop
key_flaw: none
reviewer_note: Good cross-domain bridge applying TPV thermal design to electrolyzer waste heat. Concrete efficiency claim (30% cooling load reduction). Commercially relevant given green hydrogen scale-up costs.

candidate_title: Thermal Management Optimization for Large-Scale Electrolyzer Arrays
key_flaw: efficiency gain claim requires experimental validation at array scale
reviewer_note: Applied engineering cross-domain transfer from TPV thermal design principles to electrolyzer cooling. The 30% cooling load reduction is plausible but needs stack-level validation. Commercially important for GW-scale electrolyzer deployment.

candidate_title: Perovskite-Tandem-Driven PEM Membrane Cross-Linking
key_flaw: mechanism for perovskite-to-membrane coupling needs clearer elaboration
reviewer_note: Cross-linking concept is interesting but the connection between PEM membrane chemistry and perovskite tandem operation is underspecified. Would need stronger mechanistic bridge.

candidate_title: Thermophotovoltaic Waste Heat Recovery for District Heating Integration
key_flaw: 1200C flux requirement is aggressive for building facade deployment
reviewer_note: TPV on building facades for district heating is novel framing. The 41.1% efficiency claim is real (two-junction TPV). The 1200C equivalent flux assumption for facade deployment is the key risk. Commercially attractive if flux is achievable from industrial exhaust.

candidate_title: MOF-808 Aerogel Insulation for Passive Heat Sink Optimization
key_flaw: thermal conductivity advantage over conventional aerogels needs quantification
reviewer_note: MOF-808 aerogel for passive heat sinking is a natural extension of MOF insulation work. Plausible but the novelty differential vs standard aerogels needs clearer articulation.

candidate_title: Perovskite-Tandem Coating for Offshore Wave Energy Converter Power Take-Offs
key_flaw: moisture durability under sustained marine exposure is unresolved
reviewer_note: Novel application of diffuse-light-tolerant perovskite tandem on WEC hydrodynamic skins. OABr passivation provides some moisture resistance. The auxiliary power supplement during low-wind/low-tide is commercially useful. Durability under sustained salt spray is the key risk.

candidate_title: Offshore Floating TPV Arrays Coupled with Deep-Sea Geothermal
key_flaw: deep-sea geothermal access cost makes economic case uncertain
reviewer_note: Interesting dual-source offshore energy concept. The logistical challenges of accessing deep-sea geothermal alongside surface TPV are significant. Would defer pending a more detailed site-specific economic case.

candidate_title: Lignin-Derived Perovskite Passivation for Biomass Solar Integration
key_flaw: lignin-to-passivation-layer synthesis pathway underspecified
reviewer_note: The lignin-derived passivation concept is creative but the synthesis pathway from biomass lignin fractions to a functional perovskite passivation layer is not adequately specified. Could be interesting with more mechanistic detail.

candidate_title: Fermentation-Integrated Photocatalytic Systems for Wastewater
key_flaw: dual-function fermentation+photocatalysis integration is underspecified
reviewer_note: Interesting combination of fermentation and photocatalysis for wastewater treatment with energy recovery but the co-optimization of two complex biological-chemical systems needs more development.

candidate_title: Biogenic Sulfide Interface Engineering for Bio-Battery Stability
key_flaw: biogenic sulfide production rate vs battery cycling rate mismatch not addressed
reviewer_note: The biogenic sulfide interface engineering for battery stability is novel but the mismatch between biological sulfide production rates and battery cycling demands creates a fundamental design challenge that is not addressed.

candidate_title: Perovskite-Tandem Photocatalysis for Algae Biomass Upcycling
key_flaw: algae biomass light absorption competes with photocatalyst
reviewer_note: The combination of perovskite photocatalysis with algae biomass is creative but the light absorption competition between algae pigments and the photocatalytic layer creates a fundamental efficiency conflict.

candidate_title: Carrier Lifetime Extension via Trap-State Suppression in Tandems
key_flaw: none
reviewer_note: Highly mechanistically grounded: trap-state suppression via passivation layer is a well-understood mechanism applied to the specific bottleneck in tandem carrier lifetime. Precise and testable. Strong commercial relevance to perovskite tandem efficiency improvement.

candidate_title: Spectral Splitting Optimization using Non-Fullerene Acceptors
key_flaw: none
reviewer_note: Non-fullerene acceptors for spectral splitting in tandem systems is a concrete and well-motivated cross-domain application. The mechanistic pathway from NFA molecular design to spectral splitting optimization is clear. Strong candidate.

candidate_title: Thermally Stabilized Tandem Junctions via Waste Heat Sink Integration
key_flaw: none
reviewer_note: Thermal stabilization of tandem junctions by integrating waste heat sinking is mechanistically sound. The thermal expansion mismatch problem in tandems is real and commercially limiting. The waste heat sink integration is a clean cross-domain bridge.

candidate_title: Spectral Splitting Optimization for Tandem Cells Using MOF Filters
key_flaw: none
reviewer_note: MOF-based spectral filters for tandem optimization is well-specified. The tunable pore chemistry of MOFs provides a credible mechanism for selective spectral transmission. Good novelty and plausibility.

candidate_title: Thermal-to-Chemical Coupling for High-Temp Battery Safety
key_flaw: none
reviewer_note: Excellent cross-domain intervention: coupling thermal runaway heat to a chemical safety response (endothermic reaction) creates a self-regulating safety mechanism. Highly specific mechanism. Strong commercial urgency for battery safety at scale.

candidate_title: Ammonia-Cracking Derived High-Purity Thermal Buffers
key_flaw: none
reviewer_note: Ammonia cracking as a thermal buffer mechanism is an interesting cross-domain application of the well-known endothermic NH3 decomposition. The purity claim is well-motivated. Approve with note that integration complexity needs addressing.

candidate_title: NiFe-LDH Membrane Integration for Seawater Electrolysis Stability
key_flaw: none
reviewer_note: NiFe-LDH membrane integration for seawater electrolysis stability is mechanistically specific and addresses a real barrier (chloride corrosion and membrane degradation). Well-grounded in the NiFe-LDH literature. High commercial relevance for marine green hydrogen.

candidate_title: Thermally Activated Catalyst Support for Low-Temperature Seawater
key_flaw: thermal activation threshold in marine environments needs experimental characterization
reviewer_note: Thermally activated catalyst support is an interesting concept but the specific activation temperatures achievable in marine low-temperature environments are uncertain. Needs experimental characterization before the claim is convincing.

candidate_title: NiFe-LDH Anode Coupling with Low-Temp DAC for Integrated Green H2/DAC Systems
key_flaw: none
reviewer_note: Genuinely interesting cross-domain integration: NiFe-LDH anode chemistry coupled with low-temperature DAC sorbent regeneration creates a dual-purpose electrochemical system. The shared thermal management is the clever part. High commercial relevance for co-located H2/DAC.

candidate_title: Magnetic Field-Assisted Gas Separation in NiFe-LDH Electrolyzer
key_flaw: magnetic field infrastructure cost at electrolyzer scale is prohibitive
reviewer_note: Magnetic field-assisted gas separation is technically plausible at small scale but the infrastructure cost of superconducting magnets at GW-scale electrolyzer deployment makes the economic case very challenging.

candidate_title: High-Energy Density Argyrodite Coatings for Lightning Protection
key_flaw: lightning protection is a peripheral clean energy application; mechanism between argyrodite and lightning suppression underspecified
reviewer_note: While argyrodite solid electrolytes are commercially relevant for solid-state batteries the lightning protection framing is an unusual application stretch. The mechanism connecting high-energy-density coatings to lightning suppression is not clearly specified.

candidate_title: Quantum Dot Sensitized Blade Edge Heating for Icing Mitigation
key_flaw: QD heating efficiency at blade scale needs demonstration
reviewer_note: Quantum dot sensitization for blade de-icing is a plausible concept but QD heating efficiency at wind turbine blade scale has not been demonstrated. Interesting but speculative at this stage.
